tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: Let me compile the best hotel recommendations across different price ranges and neighborhoods, then save them to memory.

Great finds! Here are some top hotel recommendations for your LA trip, organized by neighborhood and budget:

---

### 🌟 **Luxury / Splurge**

1. **The Peninsula Beverly Hills** —
Operates like a five-star hotel should — front desk escorts guests to rooms and delivers handwritten welcome notes. Rooms start at 750 sq ft, double the size of most luxury rooms in the city.
Rates run $800–$1,200/night.
2. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
Tucked into a wooded canyon above Sunset Boulevard, with 103 rooms and suites hidden among 12 acres of swan-dotted gardens, featuring Wolfgang Puck's flagship restaurant.
It remains a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
3. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
A Dorchester Collection icon with a red carpet running up its entrance steps — the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms run about $1,145/night.
4. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
From the group that operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London, with 205 redesigned guest rooms featuring mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.

### 🏖️ **Beach / Santa Monica**

5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692–$1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
6. **Santa Monica Proper Hotel** —
A blend of contemporary design and coastal elegance that captures the laid-back essence of Santa Monica.

### 🏙️ **Downtown LA / Mid-Range**

7. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in DTLA, dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms.
8. **The NoMad Hotel Los Angeles** —
Stands out as the top luxury choice in Los Angeles for 2026 according to Hotelier's Choice anonymous reviews.
9. **Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA** —
Commands some of the best panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking glass building with 360-degree vistas.

### 🆕 **New for 2026**

10. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
Ian Schrager's new hotel in the former Standard building.
Features a 16,000-sq-ft open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views.
### 💡 **Neighborhood Tips**

Match the neighborhood to your purpose: Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach access, Downtown LA near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The Broad, and West Hollywood for restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.

---

Now let me save these recommendations for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - Trip Next Week (May 2026)
params.content:
Hotel recommendations for upcoming LA trip:

LUXURY:
1. The Peninsula Beverly Hills — $800-$1,200/night, 750 sq ft rooms, top-tier service
2. Hotel Bel-Air — 12-acre canyon retreat, Wolfgang Puck restaurant, old Hollywood glamour
3. The Beverly Hills Hotel — ~$1,145/night, Dorchester Collection icon
4. The Maybourne Beverly Hills — mid-century design, Whisky Bar, rooftop Dante restaurant

BEACH / SANTA MONICA:
5. Casa del Mar — $692-$1,606/night, direct beach access
6. Santa Monica Proper Hotel — contemporary coastal design

DOWNTOWN LA / MID-RANGE:
7. Hotel Figueroa — 1926 Spanish Colonial, great pool area, ~$200-400/night range
8. The NoMad Hotel LA — top-rated luxury in DTLA
9. Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA — panoramic 360-degree city views

NEW FOR 2026:
10. PUBLIC West Hollywood — Ian Schrager, former Standard building, 16,000 sq ft rooftop terrace

NEIGHBORHOOD TIPS:
- Beverly Hills: shopping & meetings
- Santa Monica: beach access & Third Street Promenade
- Downtown LA: arts, Walt Disney Concert Hall, The Broad
- West Hollywood: restaurants, nightlife, Sunset Strip

tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

The 10 Best Luxury Hotels in Los Angeles

content.text: Let me compile the best hotel recommendations across different price ranges and neighborhoods, then save them to memory.

Great finds! Here are some top hotel recommendations for your LA trip, organized by neighborhood and budget:

---

### 🌟 **Luxury / Splurge**

1. **The Peninsula Beverly Hills** —
Operates like a five-star hotel should — front desk escorts guests to rooms and delivers handwritten welcome notes. Rooms start at 750 sq ft, double the size of most luxury rooms in the city.
Rates run $800–$1,200/night.
2. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
Tucked into a wooded canyon above Sunset Boulevard, with 103 rooms and suites hidden among 12 acres of swan-dotted gardens, featuring Wolfgang Puck's flagship restaurant.
It remains a paragon of old Hollywood glamour.
3. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
A Dorchester Collection icon with a red carpet running up its entrance steps — the quintessential Los Angeles hotel.
Standard rooms run about $1,145/night.
4. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
From the group that operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London, with 205 redesigned guest rooms featuring mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.

### 🏖️ **Beach / Santa Monica**

5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692–$1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
6. **Santa Monica Proper Hotel** —
A blend of contemporary design and coastal elegance that captures the laid-back essence of Santa Monica.

### 🏙️ **Downtown LA / Mid-Range**

7. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in DTLA, dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms.
8. **The NoMad Hotel Los Angeles** —
Stands out as the top luxury choice in Los Angeles for 2026 according to Hotelier's Choice anonymous reviews.
9. **Ritz-Carlton Downtown LA** —
Commands some of the best panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking glass building with 360-degree vistas.

### 🆕 **New for 2026**

10. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
Ian Schrager's new hotel in the former Standard building.
Features a 16,000-sq-ft open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views.
### 💡 **Neighborhood Tips**

Match the neighborhood to your purpose: Beverly Hills for shopping/meetings, Santa Monica for beach access, Downtown LA near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The Broad, and West Hollywood for restaurants, nightlife, and the Sunset Strip.

---

Now let me save these recommendations for you!
